ops.action: Treat CLI output + generated artifacts as a contract: ensure deterministic ordering/formatting, update or add focused pytest coverage (prefer existing contract tests like tests/test_compound_install.py), and run the standard gates (uv run basedpyright, uv run ruff check ., uv run pytest <targeted>).
ops.title: Compound CLI changes must be pinned by contract tests
ops.trigger: When editing src/agent_loom/compound/cli.py (especially large refactors/simplifications) or behavior-adjacent modules like src/agent_loom/compound/sync.py

ops.action: Scan the edited docs for concrete promises (commands, flags, output snippets, workflow steps). Ensure those are still true given the code changes; if output is contract-tested, prefer docs wording that matches the contract-tested output.
ops.title: Docs touched => verify UX claims match reality
ops.trigger: When updating README.md or LOOM.md alongside functional changes

ops.action: Write down the behavior invariants (inputs/flags -> outputs/side effects), ensure each invariant is covered by at least one test, and ensure error messages/exit codes remain intentional (no accidental silent success/failure).
ops.title: Large deletions imply explicit behavior audit
ops.trigger: When a diff removes hundreds of lines from a core module (e.g. src/agent_loom/compound/cli.py) or collapses multiple flows into fewer branches

ops.body:
# Compound CLI Contract Testing

Use this when changing Compound CLI behavior (especially refactors) so UX + artifacts stay deterministic.

## Scope
- Applies to: `src/agent_loom/compound/cli.py`, `src/agent_loom/compound/sync.py` (and any Compound command wiring they influence)
- Goal: deterministic output, stable semantics, and regression coverage

## Checklist
1. Identify the contract surface
- CLI: command names, flags, help text, exit codes, stderr vs stdout
- Output: headings/labels, ordering, whitespace, and any user-visible paths
- Artifacts: any generated/installed files, rendered templates, or computed lists

2. Make determinism explicit
- Sort lists before printing
- Use stable iteration order (avoid depending on dict insertion order unless guaranteed by construction)
- Avoid timestamps/randomness in outputs unless explicitly part of the contract

3. Update/add focused contract tests
- Prefer extending existing tests that already pin Compound behavior (start by checking `tests/test_compound_install.py`)
- Test both:
  - happy path output/content
  - failure modes (bad args, missing files, partial state) with intentional messages and exit codes

4. Keep docs aligned when you touch them
- If you edit `README.md` or `LOOM.md`, verify any example commands/outputs still match the contract behavior
- Prefer language that mirrors contract-tested output when practical

## Verify (local gates)
- `uv run basedpyright`
- `uv run ruff check .`
- `uv run pytest tests/test_compound_install.py` (plus any new/adjacent tests you add)

## Done when
- Contract tests pin the behavior you intended to preserve
- Gates are green
- No accidental UX drift (output/exit codes) from the refactor
ops.description: Keep Compound CLI behavior deterministic and protected by focused contract tests when refactoring src/agent_loom/compound/cli.py or src/agent_loom/compound/sync.py.
ops.name: compound-cli-contract-testing
